title: Threshold of Light
curator_statement:
There is a moment in the late afternoon when the city belongs to neither day nor night. The facades of Alfama still hold the warmth of the sun — a deep, amber glow trapped in ceramic tile — but the neon of the shop signs has already begun to assert itself, casting cold pools of blue and pink across wet cobblestone.

This exhibition gathers twelve photographs taken during that threshold hour across two weeks in Lisbon. They are not dramatic images. Nothing decisive happens in any of them. Instead, they document a quality of light that exists for perhaps fifteen minutes each evening before the city tips fully into darkness.

What connects these works is not subject but atmosphere — the way a tiled wall becomes a screen for projected color, the way a figure crossing a square is caught between two kinds of shadow. The curator's interest here is in photography as a medium uniquely suited to recording transitions that the eye registers but rarely holds.

works.label: Alfama at dusk, October 2019
works.curator_note: The lead image — where warm and cool light meet on a single wall.

works.label: Figure crossing Praça do Comércio
works.curator_note: Caught between the last daylight and the first streetlamp.
